_comment: Provenance + scan policy for the PostgreSQL binary that the embedded-postgres dependency downloads at runtime. It lives OUTSIDE go.sum (Maven Central, not the Go module proxy), so it needs its own COMMITTED pin + scan. This binary is used both by the integration tests AND by the served single-node/eval path (internal/server/startBundledPostgres), so the pin is enforced at runtime (SUPPLY-003), not just in CI. See deploy/supply-chain/README.md and docs/supply-chain.md.

checksum.subject: linux-amd64 jar (the production single-node/eval default)
checksum.policy: committed pin (TOFU completed): scripts/supply-chain/verify-embedded-postgres.sh verifies the downloaded jar against this value on every CI run and FAILS the build on any change; internal/server/startBundledPostgres verifies the cached .txz against archives[].txz_sha256 below before starting PostgreSQL, so a tampered or MITM'd binary is refused at runtime (SUPPLY-003).

scan.policy: advisory/report-only: a transitive OS-package CVE in a prebuilt PostgreSQL binary is fixable only by bumping the pinned version upstream, so findings are recorded rather than blocking. The hard gate is the checksum-change check (above) and the runtime provenance check (SUPPLY-003).
scan.lastResult: pending first CI run on a network-enabled runner

notes: PostgreSQL 16.4 is a supported release. The jar and the inner .txz hashes above were captured from Maven Central and cross-checked against the library's same-origin .sha256 sidecar. The .txz is the artifact the embedded-postgres library caches and extracts; the runtime verifies the cached .txz against txz_sha256 before starting PostgreSQL. This dependency is NOT bundled in the shipped distroless image (which carries only the Go binaries); it is fetched on first run of the bundled single-node/eval path.
